methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
DNA Script is a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in Biotech & Health, with primary focus on 3 same-stage private comparables: Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B).

Two triangulation methods apply to this target. Stage-progression analysis, using $200M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies $340M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage companies yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range of $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the sector median P/S multiple is 6.1x across 3 public tickers, but DNA Script's revenue is not disclosed, precluding this method.

The two applicable methods produce a wide spread, implying a triangulated range of $289M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The stage-progression method anchors toward the lower bound, reflecting funding history alone. The peer-median method dominates the upper range. Key uncertainties include stage and maturity relative to named peers, revenue trajectory, and competitive positioning within the genomics sector.
